mnemonics.title: Display List Purpose
mnemonics.content: DRIP: Display list Retains Instructions for Performance - stores commands to avoid re-parsing and improve rendering speed.

mnemonics.title: Types of Display Lists
mnemonics.content: LHI: Linear - Hierarchical - Indexed - three main types to remember

acronyms.usage: Used in OpenGL functions like glNewList() and glEndList() to create and execute display lists
OpenGL uses display lists as a core feature for efficient rendering
Applications that extensively use display lists for rendering complex drawings

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a straight line of building blocks, each block representing a graphics command stacked sequentially from start to end.
Think of an organizational chart - the root at top with branches flowing down, parent nodes affecting all children below them.
Picture a tape recorder playing back a pre-recorded tape - the commands are played back exactly as recorded, without needing to re-create them.
Think of retained mode as a saved document you can reopen, while immediate mode is like typing and printing in real-time without saving.

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe card - once written, you can follow the same instructions multiple times to cook the same dish without re-writing the recipe.
Like a player piano - the display list is the piano roll (program), and the interpreter reads it to produce music (graphics output).
Like a family tree - grandparents affect all descendants, parents affect their children, and transformations cascade down similar to inheritance.
Like a painted canvas - once the picture is painted (display list created), you can view it repeatedly without repainting.
